description_long: This object consists of several glass bottles arranged on a wooden surface, likely part of a dining or bar setting. The bottles are cylindrical with narrow necks and are accompanied by Japanese labels, suggesting they may contain sake or similar beverages. The bottles are grouped closely together, with visible caps and some variation in label design and color. The glass appears to be clear or lightly tinted, and the labels are predominantly white with black and gold accents. The bottles are surrounded by other dining items, including a plate of food and small cups, indicating a social or restaurant context. The overall appearance is clean, with no visible damage or wear on the bottles.
description: A group of clear glass bottles with Japanese labels, arranged on a wooden surface in a dining setting. The bottles are clean, cylindrical, and capped, with visible white, black, and gold label designs.
description_view_0: Multiple glass bottles with Japanese labels are visible behind a plate of food, showing the front of the labels and caps.
description_view_1: Side view of the bottles, with labels partially visible and the cylindrical shape of the bottles more apparent.
description_view_2: Rear view of the bottles, showing mostly the backs of the labels and the tops of the caps.
description_view_3: Angled view showing the bottles from the side, with some label details and the arrangement on the wooden surface.
attribute_label_or_branding: Japanese text and branding on white paper labels with black and gold accents, placed vertically on the bottle bodies.
